articles.title: Weekly poll: would you buy the Oppo Reno16? What about the Oppo Reno16 Pro?
articles.description: Oppo unveiled the Reno16 series this week – this includes the vanilla and Pro models as well as the Reno16 F and FS versions. Let’s focus on the first pair – do you like what the Reno16 and Reno16 Pro have to offer? And have you seen how much they cost? Oppo Reno 16 and Reno 16 Pro We will need to re-calibrate our idea of how much a mid-range phone costs. The Oppo Reno16 starts at €900 for an 8/512GB phone, though you can get a €100 discount if you order before the end of July. The phone is powered by a Snapdragon 7 Gen 4 and a 6,700mAh battery with 80W fast charging (55W if...

articles.title: Sunday Reboot: Apple's gameshow phobia won't change anytime soon
articles.description: In this week's "Sunday Reboot," Malcolm ponders why Apple TV doesn't do game shows, and if it ever will properly work competition-based shows into its streaming service. Planet of the Apps, Apple's first attempt to make a proper game show. Image Credit: Apple Anyone paying a subscription expects to get their money's worth from their monthly outlay, especially when it comes to streaming services. I occasionally look at the collection that I pay for each month, and if I haven't watched it enough in the last few months, it gets cancelled for a while. This does help save a bit of money, but the one that I simply cannot do this to is Apple TV. That's primarily because it's in my Apple One subscription and I use everything else in the package a lot. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: Samsung Galaxy A37 and Galaxy A57 are available at a discounted price in the UK
articles.description: The Samsung Galaxy A37 and Galaxy A57, which were unveiled in March and went on sale in the UK in April, are now available at a discounted price on Samsung's official UK website until July 14. The Galaxy A37 was launched in the UK with 6GB/128GB and 8GB/256GB memory configurations priced at £399 and £459, respectively. The Galaxy A57, on the other hand, launched with 8GB/256GB and 12GB/512GB options, priced at £529 and £699, respectively. articles.title: UltraProlink launches Boost 45 MP, 45W GaN travel charger
articles.description: UltraProlink has unveiled the Boost 45 MP, a compact 45W GaN III travel charger designed for modern professionals. Featuring dual USB-C ports and broad multi-protocol support, it efficiently charges smartphones, tablets, laptops, and more from various brands with a single device. Priced at ₹1,599, this BIS-certified charger includes a 1-meter USB-C cable and offers robust protection for global travel.

articles.title: Italy launches probe into Microsoft over automatic upgrade to costlier AI subscriptions
articles.description: Italy's antitrust watchdog has launched an investigation into Microsoft over its recent price hikes for the Microsoft 365 subscription service. The authority alleges the tech giant failed to clearly inform consumers about the inclusion of new AI features like Copilot and Designer, automatically upgrading users to more expensive plans. This move, criticized for insufficient consumer information and potentially restricting choice, is being examined for aggressive commercial practices.

articles.title: EU stands firm on digital tax, tells US President Donald Trump: ‘We have the ...’
articles.description: The European Union has firmly rejected US President Donald Trump's threats regarding digital services taxes. Brussels asserts its sovereign right to tax economic activity within its borders, stating these taxes apply equally to all large companies, irrespective of origin. Despite Trump's vow of retaliatory tariffs, the EU remains committed to its policies while seeking a global resolution.

articles.title: Forget ChatGPT? China's cheaper AI models are quietly taking over enterprise AI, says Jefferies
articles.description: The global artificial intelligence race may be entering a new phase—one where the biggest competitive advantage is no longer superior performance but dramatically lower costs. According to Jefferies, China's latest AI model, GLM-5.2, could become a bigger threat to Western AI leaders than DeepSeek by reshaping the enterprise AI market.

articles.title: AI replaces some entry-level jobs, but employers say communication, leadership and professionalism will decide who gets hired: Survey
articles.description: Artificial intelligence is reshaping entry-level hiring by automating routine tasks, but employers continue to value graduates with strong human skills, according to the GMAC Corporate Recruiters Survey 2026. While sectors like consulting, finance and accounting are seeing increased AI adoption, hiring demand for MBA and business master's graduates remains positive. Employers increasingly prioritise communication, leadership, professionalism, adaptability and strategic thinking, encouraging students to combine technical expertise with AI literacy and interpersonal skills for long-term career success.

articles.description: Renault is set to launch the updated Kwid hatchback on July 3, marking its first significant update in years. The facelift features design elements inspired by the Dacia Spring EV including a new closed-off grille and Y-shaped LED daytime running lights.

articles.title: NEET UG 2026 result date and time: Check expected release date, how to download scorecard, official websites and latest NTA update
articles.description: NEET UG 2026 results are eagerly awaited by candidates following the Re-NEET examination on June 21. The National Testing Agency is processing answer key challenges and will soon release scorecards, All India Ranks, and merit lists. While an official date is pending, results are typically announced 30-40 days post-exam, suggesting a July 2026 release. Candidates should monitor official NTA and NEET websites for verified updates.

articles.title: Where are the unicorns of 2021
articles.description: In 2021, India witnessed a surge of 39 startups achieving unicorn status. Following a period of easy funding, these companies, including Cred and Meesho, now navigate a changed landscape marked by global uncertainties and investor caution. While some, like Cred, continue to secure significant investments, others are adapting their strategies or pursuing public listings, reflecting the evolving dynamics of the startup ecosystem.

articles.title: Chinese and Japanese companies launch AI models, claim they are as powerful as Anthropic’s Mythos
articles.description: Japan and China have unveiled advanced AI models for cybersecurity, challenging US tech dominance. Sakana AI's 'Fugu' and 360's 'Tulongfeng' and 'Yitianzhen' offer capabilities comparable to restricted US systems like Anthropic's. These launches follow US export controls, highlighting a growing regional push for independent AI infrastructure and potentially impacting the lucrative global AI market.

articles.title: Google Wallet can now track your packages
articles.description: While probably not the most obvious or sensible integration out there, it does sound like a pretty nifty one. Also, we are pretty certain that this is not the first time Google has experimented with package tracking. If memory serves right, the now-retired Google Now (or rather transformed into Google Discover feed) used to offer the very same thing. Anyway, the idea is simple – Google scans your Gmail inbox and automatically recognizes digital receipts and tracking numbers, then simply uses those to provide parcel tracking info as part of the Google Wallet interface. The UI places...

articles.title: Weekly Horoscope, June 28 to July 4, 2026: Read weekly astrological predictions
articles.description: Your weekly horoscope is here to guide you through life's journey. Discover what the stars have in store for Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricorn, Aquarius, and Pisces. Each sign receives insights into their professional life, relationships, finances, and health for the upcoming week. Navigate challenges and embrace opportunities with celestial wisdom.

articles.title: Ambient intelligence: How AI is becoming omnipresent in our lives and the implications
articles.description: Ambient intelligence, once a futuristic concept, is now a consumer reality with AI embedded in everyday devices like smart glasses and rings. This technology anticipates user needs, shifting from reactive queries to proactive assistance. While offering potential benefits, it raises significant concerns about privacy, consent, and the erosion of human agency, potentially leading to a future where technology demands surrender rather than attention.
